mnemonics.title: WATER Quality Parameters
mnemonics.content: WATER stands for: pH, Temperature, Turbidity, Dissolved Oxygen, Color, Odor, Taste, Residues (TDS), Iron/Manganese, Coliform count

mnemonics.title: Hydrological Cycle Steps
mnemonics.content: PRECIPITATION flows as RUNOFF, INFILTRATES into soil, PERCOLATES to groundwater, EVAPORATES and TRANSPIRES back to clouds

mnemonics.title: BOD vs COD Difference
mnemonics.content: BOD is 'Biological' (uses microbes, slower, 5 days); COD (uses dichromate, faster is 'Chemical', measures all oxidizable matter)

acronyms.usage: Measures organic pollution by oxygen needed for microbial decomposition
Measures total oxidizable matter using chemical oxidants
Oxygen available in water for aquatic life; indicator of water quality
Dissolved minerals, salts, metals in water; affects taste and health
Standard unit for measuring water turbidity/cloudiness
Particles suspended in water; contributes to turbidity

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a circular conveyor belt: Sun evaporates ocean → clouds form → rain falls on mountains → water flows as rivers → returns to ocean. This continuous循环 (circulation) never ends.

Draw a sandwich: Top bread = soil, Cheese = unconfined aquifer (exposed to air), Bottom bread = impermeable rock. For confined: Top bread = aquiclude, Cheese = confined aquifer under pressure, Bottom bread = aquiclude.
Think of a number line: 0 (battery acid) → 7 (pure water, center) → 14 (drain cleaner). Colors: Red (acidic) → Green (neutral) → Blue/Purple (alkaline). Most fish need middle zone.
Imagine two water bottles: Clean river (BOD <5) = fish swimming happily; Sewage water (BOD >200) = fish floating upside down because oxygen is 'eaten' by bacteria decomposing waste.

analogies.analogy: Like filling a sponge (aquifer) with water. The sponge holds water but doesn't let it pass through until squeezed (pumped).
Like a kitchen sink: All water from different parts of the counter (land) flows to the drain (river outlet). The sink edges (divides) separate where water goes.
Like measuring how many guests (bacteria) eat at a party (water sample). More food (organic waste) = more guests eat = faster food disappears = higher BOD.
Check dam is like a speed bump (slows water, prevents erosion); Large dam is like a big reservoir (stores water for use).
